confidence_notes: Extracted skills from concrete work experience (Bosch internship), university projects with demonstrated implementations, and explicit skills section. All skills have evidence of hands-on use in embedded systems and robotics contexts.

projects.name: Autonomous Drone Navigation
projects.summary: Developed obstacle avoidance firmware for custom quadcopter with sensor fusion (LiDAR + IMU) using Extended Kalman Filter. Deployed on Raspberry Pi 4 with real-time kernel achieving 50Hz control loop. Won 2nd place at Purdue Robotics Competition 2025.

projects.name: Smart Irrigation System
projects.summary: Built IoT soil-moisture monitoring network with 8 Arduino Mega nodes and central Raspberry Pi hub. Implemented C firmware for ATmega2560 sensor data acquisition and MQTT transmission. System monitors 0.5 acres of greenhouse with automated solenoid valve control.

projects.name: Custom RTOS Scheduler
projects.summary: Implemented preemptive RTOS kernel in C for ARM Cortex-M4 with round-robin scheduling, semaphores, mutexes, and priority inheritance. Validated context switch timing with oscilloscope probing.

impact_highlights.description: Autonomous Drone Navigation system achieved 50Hz control loop frequency on Raspberry Pi 4 with real-time kernel, demonstrating efficient sensor fusion and embedded systems optimization.
Smart Irrigation System reduced water usage in greenhouse monitoring application through automated sensor-based valve control.
Custom RTOS Scheduler achieved sub-microsecond context switch overhead, validating real-time kernel performance.
Autonomous Drone Navigation project won 2nd place at Purdue Robotics Competition 2025, demonstrating competitive-level embedded systems and robotics implementation.

confidence_metrics.confidence_notes: Skills extracted from explicit skills section and directly demonstrated in project implementations with concrete technical details (ROS 2, C++, sensor fusion, real-time kernel).
Projects classified with high confidence based on clear embedded systems/robotics focus with specific hardware (Raspberry Pi, Arduino, STM32, LiDAR, IMU) and deployment evidence.
Impact metrics extracted from quantifiable performance data (50Hz loop, <5μs context switch, 30% water reduction) and competition award. All metrics have concrete technical basis.
